message.content:
I'm planning a learning tutorial for the SURF VHDL FPGA framework located at /home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/slaclab-surf.

Explore the `protocols/` subsystem thoroughly (medium-to-thorough breadth). I need concrete file paths for writing tutorial lectures.

For each protocol, list the key RTL files (especially `*Pkg.vhd` package files and main modules `.vhd`) with a one-line description:
1. protocols/ssi/rtl/ — SSI (Streaming Serial Interface) records, framing, SOF/EOF/EOFE sideband, SsiPkg, SsiFifo, SsiPrbsRx/Tx, SsiOb/ Ib filters
2. protocols/srp/rtl/ — SRP (SLAC Register Protocol) SRPv3, request/response, SrpPkg, SrpV3Core
3. protocols/pgp/rtl/ — PGP (PCIe/Protocol data path?) PGP2b, PGP3, PgpPkg, Pgp2bAxi, link, VC virtual channels
4. protocols/rssi/rtl/ — RSSI reliable streaming, RssiPkg, RssiCore
5. protocols/coaxpress/rtl/ — CoaXPress frame/core
6. protocols/jesd204b/rtl/ — JESD204B
7. protocols/i2c/, protocols/spi/, protocols/uart/ — peripheral control cores
8. protocols/packetizer/ and protocols/batcher/ — data framing/protection helpers

Also note the directory split convention (core/ vs family PHY wrappers) and the sideband fields that must be preserved (TKEEP/TLAST/TUSER/SOF/EOFE/VC). Give relative paths from repo root.

Report a concise structured list: protocol -> key files with descriptions and representative module names a learner should study. Do not dump full file contents.

attachment.content:
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

17	
18	library ieee;
19	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
20	
21	library surf;
22	use surf.StdRtlPkg.all;
23	use surf.AxiStreamPkg.all;
24	use surf.SsiPkg.all;
25	
26	package Pgp2bPkg is
27	
28	   -----------------------------------------------------
29	   -- Constants
30	   -----------------------------------------------------
31	   constant SSI_PGP2B_CONFIG_C : AxiStreamConfigType := ssiAxiStreamConfig(2, TKEEP_COMP_C);
32	
33	   -- 8B10B Characters
34	   constant K_COM_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "10111100";  -- K28.5, 0xBC
35	   constant K_LTS_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "00111100";  -- K28.1, 0x3C
36	   constant D_102_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "01001010";  -- D10.2, 0x4A
37	   constant D_215_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "10110101";  -- D21.5, 0xB5
38	   constant K_SKP_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "00011100";  -- K28.0, 0x1C
39	   constant K_OTS_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "01111100";  -- K28.3, 0x7C
40	   constant K_ALN_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "11011100";  -- K28.6, 0xDC
41	   constant K_SOC_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "11111011";  -- K27.7, 0xFB
42	   constant K_SOF_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "11110111";  -- K23.7, 0xF7
43	   constant K_EOF_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "11111101";  -- K29.7, 0xFD
44	   constant K_EOFE_C : slv(7 downto 0) := "11111110";  -- K30.7, 0xFE
45	   constant K_EOC_C  : slv(7 downto 0) := "01011100";  -- K28.2, 0x5C
46	
47	   -- ID Constant
48	   constant PGP2B_ID_C : slv(3 downto 0) := "0101";
49	
50	   -----------------------------------------------------
51	   -- PGP RX non-data types
52	   -----------------------------------------------------
53	
54	   type Pgp2bRxInType is record
55	      flush    : sl;                    -- Flush the link
56	      resetRx  : sl;
57	      loopback : slv(2 downto 0);
58	   end record Pgp2bRxInType;
59	
60	   type Pgp2bRxInArray is array (natural range <>) of Pgp2bRxInType;
61	
62	   constant PGP2B_RX_IN_INIT_C : Pgp2bRxInType := (
63	      flush    => '0',
64	      resetRx  => '0',
65	      loopback => "000");
66	
67	   type Pgp2bRxOutType is record
68	      phyRxReady   : sl;                -- RX Phy is ready
69	      linkReady    : sl;                -- Local side has link
70	      linkPolarity : slv(1 downto 0);   -- Receive link polarity
71	      frameRx      : sl;                -- A good frame was received
72	      frameRxErr   : sl;                -- An errored frame was received
73	      cellError    : sl;                -- A cell error has occurred
74	      linkDown     : sl;                -- A link down event has occurred
75	      linkError    : sl;                -- A link error has occurred
76	      opCodeEn     : sl;                -- Opcode receive enable
77	      opCode       : slv(7 downto 0);   -- Opcode receive value
78	      remLinkReady : sl;                -- Far end side has link
79	      remLinkData  : slv(7 downto 0);   -- Far end side User Data
80	      remOverflow  : slv(3 downto 0);   -- Far end overflow status
81	      remPause     : slv(3 downto 0);   -- Far end pause status
82	   end record Pgp2bRxOutType;
83	
84	   type Pgp2bRxOutArray is array (natural range <>) of Pgp2bRxOutType;
85	
86	   constant PGP2B_RX_OUT_INIT_C : Pgp2bRxOutType := (
87	      phyRxReady   => '0',
88	      linkReady    => '0',
89	      linkPolarity => "00",
90	      frameRx      => '0',
91	      frameRxErr   => '0',
92	      cellError    => '0',
93	      linkDown     => '0',
94	      linkError    => '0',
95	      opCodeEn     => '0',
96	      opCode       => (others => '0'),
97	      remLinkReady => '0',
98	      remLinkData  => (others => '0'),
99	      remOverflow  => (others => '0'),
100	      remPause     => (others => '0'));
101	
102	   -----------------------------------------------------
103	   -- PGP2B TX non-data types
104	   -----------------------------------------------------
105	
106	   type Pgp2bTxInType is record
107	      flush       : sl;                 -- Flush the link
108	      opCodeEn    : sl;                 -- Opcode receive enable
109	      opCode      : slv(7 downto 0);    -- Opcode receive value
110	      locData     : slv(7 downto 0);    -- Near end side User Data
111	      flowCntlDis : sl;                 -- Ignore flow control
112	      resetTx     : sl;                 -- Reset tx phy
113	      resetGt     : sl;
114	   end record Pgp2bTxInType;
115	
116	   type Pgp2bTxInArray is array (natural range <>) of Pgp2bTxInType;
117	
118	   constant PGP2B_TX_IN_INIT_C : Pgp2bTxInType := (
119	      flush       => '0',
120	      opCodeEn    => '0',
121	      opCode      => (others => '0'),
122	      locData     => (others => '0'),
123	      flowCntlDis => '0',
124	      resetTx     => '0',
125	      resetGt     => '0');
126	
127	   constant PGP2B_TX_IN_HALF_DUPLEX_C : Pgp2bTxInType := (
128	      flush       => '0',
129	      opCodeEn    => '0',
130	      opCode      => (others => '0'),
131	      locData     => (others => '0'),
132	      flowCntlDis => '1',
133	      resetTx     => '0',
134	      resetGt     => '0');
135	
136	   type Pgp2bTxOutType is record
137	      locOverflow : slv(3 downto 0);    -- Local overflow status
138	      locPause    : slv(3 downto 0);    -- Local pause status
139	      phyTxReady  : sl;                 -- TX Phy is ready
140	      linkReady   : sl;                 -- Local side has link
141	      frameTx     : sl;                 -- A good frame was transmitted
142	      frameTxErr  : sl;                 -- An errored frame was transmitted
143	   end record Pgp2bTxOutType;
144	
145	   type Pgp2bTxOutArray is array (natural range <>) of Pgp2bTxOutType;
146	
147	   constant PGP2B_TX_OUT_INIT_C : Pgp2bTxOutType := (
148	      locOverflow => (others => '0'),
149	      locPause    => (others => '0'),
150	      phyTxReady  => '0',
151	      linkReady   => '0',
152	      frameTx     => '0',
153	      frameTxErr  => '0');

---

# SURF Protocols Subsystem - Tutorial Reference Guide

## Sideband Signals Convention (All Protocols)

All SURF protocols use AXI-Stream sideband signals defined in `SsiPkg`:
- **TKEEP** - Byte strobes indicating valid bytes
- **TLAST** - End of Frame (EOF) marker
- **TUSER(0)** - EOFE (End of Frame with Error) flag
- **TUSER(1)** - SOF (Start of Frame) flag
- **TDEST** - Virtual Channel (VC) identifier (4-bit, 16 VCs max)
- **TID** - Transaction identifier (optional, protocol-dependent)

---

## 1. SSI (Streaming Serial Interface)
**Directory:** `protocols/ssi/rtl/`

| File | Description | Key Module/Type |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `SsiPkg.vhd` | Core package - AXI-Stream config, SsiMasterType/SsiSlaveType records, sideband access functions | `ssiAxiStreamConfig()`, `ssiGetUserEofe()`, `ssiGetUserSof()`, `SsiMasterType` |
| `SsiFifo.vhd` | SSI-aware FIFO with frame boundary preservation | `SsiFifo` |
| `SsiPrbsRx.vhd` | PRBS test pattern receiver with frame checking | `SsiPrbsRx` |
| `SsiPrbsTx.vhd` | PRBS test pattern transmitter | `SsiPrbsTx` |
| `SsiIbFrameFilter.vhd` | Inbound frame filter by VC/address | `SsiIbFrameFilter` |
| `SsiObFrameFilter.vhd` | Outbound frame filter | `SsiObFrameFilter` |
| `SsiFrameLimiter.vhd` | Frame size/byte count limiter | `SsiFrameLimiter` |
| `SsiInsertSof.vhd` | Insert SOF markers on frames | `SsiInsertSof` |
| `SsiAxiLiteMaster.vhd` | AXI-Lite master using SSI framing | `SsiAxiLiteMaster` |
| `SsiCmdMaster.vhd` | Command master module | `SsiCmdMaster` |

## Directory Split Convention

The SURF protocols subsystem follows a consistent pattern:
```
protocols/
├── {protocol}/
│   ├── rtl/              # Core RTL (technology-independent)
│   ├── tb/               # Testbench files
│   ├── wrappers/         # Example wrappers (optional)
│   └── {family}/         # PHY-specific implementations
│       ├── rtl/          # Family-specific PHY wrappers
```

**PHY Family Naming Convention:**
- `gtp7` - Xilinx 7-series GTP
- `gtx7` - Xilinx 7-series GTX
- `gth7` - Xilinx 7-series GTH
- `gthUs` - Xilinx UltraScale GTH
- `gthUs+` - Xilinx UltraScale+ GTH
- `gtyUs+` - Xilinx UltraScale+ GTY
- `7Series` - 7-series specific
- `UltraScale` - UltraScale specific
